author: e-ddykim
title: EasyOCR model download fails with HTTP 504 Gateway Time-out in PyTorch Models tests
signature: HTTPError: HTTP Error 504: Gateway Time-out|External Service

failed_jobs.name: PyTorch Models tests / PyTorch Models tests
failed_jobs.symptom: 4 EasyOCR tests fail: HTTP 504 when downloading model weights via easyocr.Reader()

key_errors:
HTTPError: HTTP Error 504: Gateway Time-out
  File "/venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/easyocr/easyocr.py", line 178, in __init__
    download_and_unzip(model['url'], model['filename'], ...)
  File "/venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/easyocr/utils.py", line 628, in download_and_unzip
    urlretrieve(url, zip_path, reporthook=reporthook)

FAILED install/tests/model_hub_tests/pytorch/test_easyocr.py::TestEasyOCRConvertModel::test_convert_model[CPU-detector]
FAILED install/tests/model_hub_tests/pytorch/test_easyocr.py::TestEasyOCRConvertModel::test_convert_model[CPU-recognizer]
FAILED install/tests/model_hub_tests/pytorch/test_easyocr.py::TestEasyOCRConvertModel::test_convert_model_export[CPU-detector]
FAILED install/tests/model_hub_tests/pytorch/test_easyocr.py::TestEasyOCRConvertModel::test_convert_model_export[CPU-recognizer]
4 failed, 8 passed in 236.46s
root_cause: The EasyOCR library (test_easyocr.py) attempts to download model weights from a remote URL at test time via urllib.request.urlretrieve. The download server returned HTTP 504 Gateway Time-out, indicating the upstream model hosting server was temporarily unavailable or overloaded. This is a transient external service failure unrelated to the PR changes ([GPU] correct SDPA micro prefetch bounds).
reproduction_steps: N/A - transient external service failure that cannot be reproduced locally on demand
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed jobs: the HTTP 504 is transient and a retry is expected to succeed
Long-term: pre-cache EasyOCR model weights on the CI runner or in the shared cache volume (/mount/caches/) to avoid runtime downloads
prevention_strategies: Cache EasyOCR model files in the CI runner's shared cache directory (e.g., /mount/caches/easyocr/) and configure the EASYOCR_MODULE_PATH environment variable to point to the cache. This eliminates the dependency on external model hosting during CI runs.
historical_context: First occurrence of this specific EasyOCR HTTP 504 pattern in the database. Similar external-service download failures have been seen with other model hub tests.
